title: Candidate pairwise query-conditioned token stats

queries: token similarity statistics candidate scorer scalable multiclass ranking
query conditioned token interaction non autoregressive candidate ranking

evidence.why_now: EXP138 showed that static position-aware label pooling is too weak, and EXP141 showed that even sample-conditioned single-vector pooling still leaves item-incoterms in the same failed band. EXP099 remains the strongest signal that candidate-token discrimination matters. The next justified scalable step is therefore to keep the cheap pairwise scorer but preserve token-level similarity statistics in the final score, instead of collapsing each candidate view to one vector too early.

hypothesis: If the missing scalable signal is carried by token-level similarity peaks and spread rather than by a single pooled label vector, then augmenting the fast pairwise scorer with query-conditioned token-statistics residuals should recover part of the salt-side candidate-token benefit while staying in a non-autoregressive O(L + C*T) regime that remains plausible for Stage 4 pretraining.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This family keeps the fast pairwise scorer and query-conditioned token attention, but additionally preserves mean and max token-similarity statistics as an explicit residual in the candidate score.
The intended benefit is to retain more of the useful candidate-token discrimination from the hybrid line without returning to autoregressive candidate rollout.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Completed on 2026-05-09 as implementation-blocked / abnormal-run rather than scientifically resolved.

EXP145 finished with a strong user-ltv result, but EXP144 was externally terminated by SIGTERM before any evaluation and EXP146 crashed immediately because the new token-stats path did not initialize its query/key projections.
Do not use EXP144 as scientific evidence against the token-stats family; rerun cleanly after the projection-init fix.
